title: Panax papyrifer
intro: Panax papyrifer, also known as the paper mulberry or Chinese paper mulberry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Moraceae. The roots of Panax papyrifer have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Panax papyrifer include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the roots of Panax papyrifer are often made into decoctions, tinctures, or infusions, and are also used to make a herbal tea, known as "li lu" or "white mulberry root", which is used to promote health and well-being.
